item.title: LaSER: Internalizing Explicit Reasoning into Latent Space for Dense Retrieval
item.description: LLMs have fundamentally transformed dense retrieval, upgrading backbones from discriminative encoders to generative architectures. However, a critical disconnect remains: while LLMs possess strong reasoning capabilities, current retrieve...

item.title: RubricBench: Aligning Model-Generated Rubrics with Human Standards
item.description: As Large Language Model (LLM) alignment evolves from simple completions to complex, highly sophisticated generation, Reward Models are increasingly shifting toward rubric-guided evaluation to mitigate surface-level biases. However, the c...

item.title: CHIMERA: Compact Synthetic Data for Generalizable LLM Reasoning
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently exhibited remarkable reasoning capabilities, largely enabled by supervised fine-tuning (SFT)- and reinforcement learning (RL)-based post-training on high-quality reasoning data. However, reprodu...

item.title: LLaDA-o: An Effective and Length-Adaptive Omni Diffusion Model
item.description: We present LLaDA-o, an effective and length-adaptive omni diffusion model for multimodal understanding and generation. LLaDA-o is built on a Mixture of Diffusion (MoD) framework that decouples discrete masked diffusion for text understan...

item.title: Efficient RLVR Training via Weighted Mutual Information Data Selection
item.description: Reinforcement learning (RL) plays a central role in improving the reasoning and alignment of large language models, yet its efficiency critically depends on how training data are selected. Existing online selection strategies predominant...

item.title: ArtLLM: Generating Articulated Assets via 3D LLM
item.description: Creating interactive digital environments for gaming, robotics, and simulation relies on articulated 3D objects whose functionality emerges from their part geometry and kinematic structure. However, existing approaches remain fundamental...

item.title: RAISE: Requirement-Adaptive Evolutionary Refinement for Training-Free Text-to-Image Alignment
item.description: Recent text-to-image (T2I) diffusion models achieve remarkable realism, yet faithful prompt-image alignment remains challenging, particularly for complex prompts with multiple objects, relations, and fine-grained attributes. Existing tra...

item.title: Classroom Final Exam: An Instructor-Tested Reasoning Benchmark
item.description: We introduce (Classroom Final Exam), a multimodal benchmark for evaluating the reasoning capabilities of large language models across more than 20 STEM domains. is curated from repeatedly used, authentic university homework and exam prob...

item.title: From Scale to Speed: Adaptive Test-Time Scaling for Image Editing
item.description: Image Chain-of-Thought (Image-CoT) is a test-time scaling paradigm that improves image generation by extending inference time. Most Image-CoT methods focus on text-to-image (T2I) generation. Unlike T2I generation, image editing is goal-d...

item.title: MMR-Life: Piecing Together Real-life Scenes for Multimodal Multi-image Reasoning
item.description: Recent progress in the reasoning capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) has empowered them to address more complex tasks such as scientific analysis and mathematical reasoning. Despite their promise, MLLMs' reasoning ab...

item.title: CoVe: Training Interactive Tool-Use Agents via Constraint-Guided Verification
item.description: Developing multi-turn interactive tool-use agents is challenging because real-world user needs are often complex and ambiguous, yet agents must execute deterministic actions to satisfy them. To address this gap, we introduce CoVe (Constr...

item.title: Tool-R0: Self-Evolving LLM Agents for Tool-Learning from Zero Data
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) are becoming the foundation for autonomous agents that can use tools to solve complex tasks. Reinforcement learning (RL) has emerged as a common approach for injecting such agentic capabilities, but typically...

item.title: Unified Vision-Language Modeling via Concept Space Alignment
item.description: We introduce V-SONAR, a vision-language embedding space extended from the text-only embedding space SONAR (Omnilingual Embeddings Team et al., 2026), which supports 1500 text languages and 177 speech languages. To construct V-SONAR, we p...

item.title: FireRed-OCR Technical Report
item.description: We present FireRed-OCR, a systematic framework to specialize general VLMs into high-performance OCR models. Large Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated impressive general capabilities but frequently suffer from ``structural hal...

item.title: MicroVerse: A Preliminary Exploration Toward a Micro-World Simulation
item.description: Recent advances in video generation have opened new avenues for macroscopic simulation of complex dynamic systems, but their application to microscopic phenomena remains largely unexplored. Microscale simulation holds great promise for b...

item.title: SWE-rebench V2: Language-Agnostic SWE Task Collection at Scale
item.description: Software engineering agents (SWE) are improving rapidly, with recent gains largely driven by reinforcement learning (RL). However, RL training is constrained by the scarcity of large-scale task collections with reproducible execution env...

item.title: OpenAutoNLU: Open Source AutoML Library for NLU
item.description: OpenAutoNLU is an open-source automated machine learning library for natural language understanding (NLU) tasks, covering both text classification and named entity recognition (NER). Unlike existing solutions, we introduce data-aware tra...

item.title: CMI-RewardBench: Evaluating Music Reward Models with Compositional Multimodal Instruction
item.description: While music generation models have evolved to handle complex multimodal inputs mixing text, lyrics, and reference audio, evaluation mechanisms have lagged behind. In this paper, we bridge this critical gap by establishing a comprehensive...

item.title: PhotoBench: Beyond Visual Matching Towards Personalized Intent-Driven Photo Retrieval
item.description: Personal photo albums are not merely collections of static images but living, ecological archives defined by temporal continuity, social entanglement, and rich metadata, which makes the personalized photo retrieval non-trivial. However,...

item.title: Learn Hard Problems During RL with Reference Guided Fine-tuning
item.description: Reinforcement learning (RL) for mathematical reasoning can suffer from reward sparsity: for challenging problems, LLM fails to sample any correct trajectories, preventing RL from receiving meaningful positive feedback. At the same time,...

item.title: When Does RL Help Medical VLMs? Disentangling Vision, SFT, and RL Gains
item.description: Reinforcement learning (RL) is increasingly used to post-train medical Vision-Language Models (VLMs), yet it remains unclear whether RL improves medical visual reasoning or mainly sharpens behaviors already induced by supervised fine-tun...

item.title: Legal RAG Bench: an end-to-end benchmark for legal RAG
item.description: We introduce Legal RAG Bench, a benchmark and evaluation methodology for assessing the end-to-end performance of legal RAG systems. As a benchmark, Legal RAG Bench consists of 4,876 passages from the Victorian Criminal Charge Book alongs...

item.title: CC-VQA: Conflict- and Correlation-Aware Method for Mitigating Knowledge Conflict in Knowledge-Based Visual Question Answering
item.description: Knowledge-based visual question answering (KB-VQA) demonstrates significant potential for handling knowledge-intensive tasks. However, conflicts arise between static parametric knowledge in vision language models (VLMs) and dynamically r...

item.title: Spectral Condition for μP under Width-Depth Scaling
item.description: Generative foundation models are increasingly scaled in both width and depth, posing significant challenges for stable feature learning and reliable hyperparameter (HP) transfer across model sizes. While maximal update parameterization (...

item.title: WorldStereo: Bridging Camera-Guided Video Generation and Scene Reconstruction via 3D Geometric Memories
item.description: Recent advances in foundational Video Diffusion Models (VDMs) have yielded significant progress. Yet, despite the remarkable visual quality of generated videos, reconstructing consistent 3D scenes from these outputs remains challenging,...

item.title: Agentic Code Reasoning
item.description: Can LLM agents explore codebases and reason about code semantics without executing the code? We study this capability, which we call agentic code reasoning, and introduce semi-formal reasoning: a structured prompting methodology that req...

item.title: CharacterFlywheel: Scaling Iterative Improvement of Engaging and Steerable LLMs in Production
item.description: This report presents CharacterFlywheel, an iterative flywheel process for improving large language models (LLMs) in production social chat applications across Instagram, WhatsApp, and Messenger. Starting from LLaMA 3.1, we refined models...

item.title: Recursive Think-Answer Process for LLMs and VLMs
item.description: Think-Answer reasoners such as DeepSeek-R1 have made notable progress by leveraging interpretable internal reasoning. However, despite the frequent presence of self-reflective cues like "Oops!", they remain vulnerable to output errors du...

item.title: Spectral Attention Steering for Prompt Highlighting
item.description: Attention steering is an important technique for controlling model focus, enabling capabilities such as prompt highlighting, where the model prioritises user-specified text. However, existing attention steering methods require explicit s...

item.title: Reasoning Core: A Scalable Procedural Data Generation Suite for Symbolic Pre-training and Post-Training
item.description: Training on verifiable symbolic data is a promising way to expand the reasoning frontier of language models beyond what standard pre-training corpora provide. Yet existing procedural generators often rely on fixed puzzles or templates an...

item.title: Planning from Observation and Interaction
item.description: Observational learning requires an agent to learn to perform a task by referencing only observations of the performed task. This work investigates the equivalent setting in real-world robot learning where access to hand-designed rewards...

item.title: OmniLottie: Generating Vector Animations via Parameterized Lottie Tokens
item.description: OmniLottie is a versatile framework that generates high quality vector animations from multi-modal instructions. For flexible motion and visual content control, we focus on Lottie, a light weight JSON formatting for both shapes and anima...

item.title: Half-Truths Break Similarity-Based Retrieval
item.description: When a text description is extended with an additional detail, image-text similarity should drop if that detail is wrong. We show that CLIP-style dual encoders often violate this intuition: appending a plausible but incorrect object or r...

item.title: Synthetic Visual Genome 2: Extracting Large-scale Spatio-Temporal Scene Graphs from Videos
item.description: We introduce Synthetic Visual Genome 2 (SVG2), a large-scale panoptic video scene graph dataset. SVG2 contains over 636K videos with 6.6M objects, 52.0M attributes, and 6.7M relations, providing an order-of-magnitude increase in scale an...

item.title: SeeThrough3D: Occlusion Aware 3D Control in Text-to-Image Generation
item.description: We identify occlusion reasoning as a fundamental yet overlooked aspect for 3D layout-conditioned generation. It is essential for synthesizing partially occluded objects with depth-consistent geometry and scale. While existing methods can...

item.title: ProtegoFed: Backdoor-Free Federated Instruction Tuning with Interspersed Poisoned Data
item.description: Federated Instruction Tuning (FIT) enables collaborative instruction tuning of large language models across multiple organizations (clients) in a cross-silo setting without requiring the sharing of private instructions. Recent findings o...

item.title: Cryo-Bench: Benchmarking Foundation Models for Cryosphere Applications
item.description: Geo-Foundation Models (GFMs) have been evaluated across diverse Earth observation task including multiple domains and have demonstrated strong potential of producing reliable maps even with sparse labels. However, benchmarking GFMs for C...

item.title: Using Songs to Improve Kazakh Automatic Speech Recognition
item.description: Developing automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems for low-resource languages is hindered by the scarcity of transcribed corpora. This proof-of-concept study explores songs as an unconventional yet promising data source for Kazakh ASR...

item.title: VGGT-Det: Mining VGGT Internal Priors for Sensor-Geometry-Free Multi-View Indoor 3D Object Detection
item.description: Current multi-view indoor 3D object detectors rely on sensor geometry that is costly to obtain (i.e., precisely calibrated multi-view camera poses) to fuse multi-view information into a global scene representation, limiting deployment in...

item.title: Tool Verification for Test-Time Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Test-time reinforcement learning (TTRL) has emerged as a promising paradigm for self-evolving large reasoning models (LRMs), enabling online adaptation on unlabeled test inputs via self-induced rewards through majority voting. However, a...

item.title: Monocular Mesh Recovery and Body Measurement of Female Saanen Goats
item.description: The lactation performance of Saanen dairy goats, renowned for their high milk yield, is intrinsically linked to their body size, making accurate 3D body measurement essential for assessing milk production potential, yet existing reconstr...
